data.narrative:
Copenhagen Atomics is a Series A nuclear energy company founded by Thomas Jam Pedersen, Peter Szabo, Thomas Steenberg, and Aslak Stubsgaard, having raised $25M+. The nuclear energy sector comp universe consists of 3 public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private peers including Fuse Energy, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Oklo.

Stage-progression methodology is the primary applicable valuation approach. Using total raised ($25M) as a proxy for post-money valuation at Series A, a 2.0x step-up multiplier to the subsequent funding stage yields an implied valuation of $50M. This reflects typical venture capital progression patterns in the frontier technology space.

Peer-median analysis is not applicable here. While 8 private nuclear energy companies exist in the broader comp set, insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage peers with comparable valuations remain after filtering—fewer than the three datapoints required for robust median analysis. Applying an all-sector median would lack sector specificity and introduce material bias.

The triangulation analysis, constrained to one reliable method, supports a valuation range of $42M to $57M, with a median of $50M. Key uncertainties include the absence of narrow-peer comparables to validate stage-progression assumptions and the nascent, heterogeneous nature of advanced nuclear development timelines. Capital efficiency relative to technical milestones remains unquantified from available data.
